title: The bag of gold which he received for the slave girl being stolen in a mosque, the young man of Baghdad tears his cloths and is about to fling himself into the Tigris, from a Tuti-nama (Tales of a Parrot): Forty-eighth Night
medium: Gum tempera, ink, and gold on paper
dimensions: Overall: 20.3 x 14 cm (8 x 5 1/2 in.); Painting only: 11.7 x 10.1 cm (4 5/8 x 4 in.)
creditLine: Cleveland Museum of Art. Gift of Mrs. A. Dean Perry
tags: Page from Tales of a Parrot (Tuti-nama): Forty-eighth night: The bag of gold which he received for the slave-girl being stolen in a mosque, the young man of Baghdad tears his cloths and is about to fling himself into the Tigris
